name: Terminus
description: Terminus was the Roman god of boundaries, landmarks, and borders. Unlike many Roman deities borrowed from the Greeks, Terminus was a distinctly Roman concept, reflecting the importance of land ownership and property rights in Roman society. He was not anthropomorphic and was typically represented by a boundary stone, often inscribed with the word 'Terminus'. Temples dedicated to him were rare; instead, his presence was acknowledged through the consecrated boundary stones themselves, which were considered sacred and inviolable. Sacrifices were offered to Terminus at these stones during the festival of Terminalia on February 23rd, a celebration that reaffirmed the boundaries of land and property. The removal or displacement of a Terminus stone was considered a grave offense, a violation of divine law punishable by severe consequences. Terminus was seen as a protector of peace and stability, ensuring the integrity of borders and preventing disputes over land. His unwavering presence symbolized the permanence and sanctity of Roman law and order. He was believed to be the only god who refused to yield to Jupiter during the construction of the Temple of Jupiter Optimus Maximus on the Capitoline Hill, further emphasizing his unyielding nature and commitment to his domain. This steadfastness made him a powerful symbol of stability and the enduring strength of Roman institutions. He embodied the Roman values of respect for tradition, the importance of land ownership, and the sanctity of boundaries.
subtitle: God of Boundaries and Landmarks

keyMyths.title: Refusal to Yield
keyMyths.description: During the construction of the Temple of Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Terminus refused to be moved, demonstrating his unwavering commitment to his domain. This act solidified his reputation as an unyielding and steadfast deity.

keyMyths.title: Terminalia Festival
keyMyths.description: The Terminalia was an annual festival held on February 23rd to honor Terminus and reaffirm the boundaries of land. Families would gather at boundary stones to offer sacrifices and celebrate the integrity of their property lines.

keyMyths.title: The Sacred Stones
keyMyths.description: Terminus was represented by sacred boundary stones, which were considered inviolable. Moving or damaging these stones was a sacrilegious act with severe consequences, emphasizing the importance of respecting boundaries.

sources.author: Livy
sources.description: A comprehensive history of Rome, offering insights into Roman religion and customs.
sources.text: A comprehensive history of Rome, offering insights into Roman religion and customs.

sources.author: Ovid
sources.description: A poetic calendar of Roman festivals, including a detailed account of the Terminalia.
sources.text: A poetic calendar of Roman festivals, including a detailed account of the Terminalia.

sources.author: Varro
